questions.question: What is the primary function of a Turing Machine?
options: A) To perform calculations
B) To simulate human thought processes
C) To store and retrieve data
D) To recognize patterns in data
questions.explanation: A Turing Machine is a theoretical model for computation that can simulate any algorithm. It reads and writes symbols on an infinite tape according to a set of rules.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a decidable language?
options: A) The set of all positive integers
B) The set of all natural numbers
C) The set of all strings of 0s and 1s
D) The set of all strings of arbitrary length
questions.explanation: The set of all positive integers is a decidable language because it can be determined whether a given number is positive or not.

questions.question: What is the difference between a deterministic finite automaton (DFA) and a nondeterministic finite automaton (NFA)?
options: A) A DFA is faster than an NFA
B) A DFA is more powerful than an NFA
C) A DFA always makes a decision, while an NFA can have multiple choices
D) A DFA always makes a choice, while an NFA always makes a decision
questions.explanation: A DFA always makes a decision based on the current state, while an NFA has multiple possible next states and may not always make a decision.

questions.question: What is the term for a set of instructions that can be used to solve a problem or perform a computation?
options: A) Algorithm
B) Protocol
C) Procedure
D) Program
questions.explanation: An algorithm is a step-by-step procedure for solving a problem or performing a computation.

questions.question: Which of the following types of automata can recognize regular languages?
options: A) DFA
B) NFA
C) Turing Machine
D) All of the above
questions.explanation: Both DFAs and NFAs can recognize regular languages. Regular languages are a class of languages that can be recognized by a finite automaton.
